mnemonics: FARM for Functional Dependencies: A Functional dependency is a Relationship between two sets of Attributes in a relation that determines one from the other.
keyPoints: Normalization is the process of organizing data to minimize redundancy and dependency.
A functional dependency (FD) is a constraint between two sets of attributes where the value of one set determines the value of the other.
Decomposition must be lossless-join and dependency-preserving to be considered a good design.
commonMistakes: Assuming all attributes in a candidate key are prime attributes (only the attributes in the primary key are considered prime).
Confusing a superkey (any superset of a candidate key) with a candidate key (a minimal superkey).
Decomposing a relation in a way that is not lossless-join, leading to a loss of information.
